Title: Parent-Based Treatment as Efficacious as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Childhood Anxiety: A Randomized Noninferiority Study of Supportive Parenting for Anxious Childhood Emotions
Authors: Eli R. Lebowitz, Carla E Marin, Alyssa Martino, Yaara Shimshoni, W. Silverman
Effect Size: Noninferior to CBT on primary and secondary anxiety outcomes.
Key Finding: SPACE was acceptable and efficacious, and noninferior to CBT.
Limitation: Original Yale/Lebowitz trial; not a replication from an independent group.
Summary: This randomized noninferiority study found SPACE to be noninferior to CBT for childhood anxiety, with reductions in family accommodation and parenting stress in both arms and greater accommodation reduction with SPACE. It is the core efficacy paper for SPACE, but it is not an independent replication because it comes from the Yale/Lebowitz line of work.

Title: The Feasibility of a Parent Group Treatment for Youth with Anxiety Disorders and Obsessive Compulsive Disorder
Authors: I. Dekel, Shirel Dorman-Ilan, C. Lang, E. Bar-David, Hadas Zilka, T. Shilton, Eli R. Lebowitz, D. Gothelf
Effect Size: Significant pre–post reductions; no controlled effect estimate in the abstract.
Key Finding: Group SPACE looked feasible and promising in a small parent sample.
Limitation: Feasibility study, not a controlled replication; still tied to Lebowitz coauthorship.
Summary: A feasibility study of group SPACE reported decreases in parental accommodation, family power struggles, parental helplessness, and anxiety/OCD severity after treatment. It is encouraging but uncontrolled, and the search did not surface it as an independent controlled replication of SPACE.
